message:
B-tree: fix parent page underflow, lock ordering, and key comparison

Three targeted fixes in the B-tree crate:

- balance.rs: Use saturating_sub() in balance_quick free-space
  calculation to prevent underflow panic when cell_content_offset
  is less than parent_used, which can happen with near-full or
  corrupt parent pages.

- cooling.rs: Swap lock acquisition order in run_cooling_scan()
  to acquire pages lock before pinned_roots, matching the lock
  ordering convention used elsewhere and preventing potential
  deadlocks. The pinned_roots lock is now held during the scan
  rather than cloned-and-dropped.

- lib.rs: Replace hand-rolled byte-by-byte comparison loop in
  compare_key_bytes_contiguous() with std library slice Ord impl
  (left.cmp(right)), which has identical semantics but is cleaner.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
